@@ -361,4 +361,1 @@ - * Returns {@code true} if this block is - * dominated by the given block {@code dom}. - * This block is dominated by {@code dom}, if every path from the root entry block to this block passes through - * {@code dom}. + * Returns {@code true} if this block is dominated by the given block {@code dom}. @@ -366,7 +363,2 @@ - * If this block, {@code b} say, and {@code dom} are not in the same parent body, - * then {@code b} becomes the nearest ancestor block, result of {@code b.ancestorBlock()}, - * and so on until either: - * {@code b} is {@code null}, therefore {@code b} is not dominated by {@code dom} and this method - * returns {@code false}; or - * {@code b.parentBody() == dom.parentBody()}, therefore this method returns the result - * of {@code b.isDominatedBy(dom)}. + * A block {@code b} is dominated by {@code dom} if every path from the entry block of {@code dom}'s + * parent body to {@code b} passes through {@code dom}. @@ -374,4 +366,11 @@ - * If this method returns {@code true} then {@code dom.isDominatedBy(this)} - * will return {@code false}. However, if this method returns {@code false} then it - * does not imply {@code dom.isDominatedBy(this)} returns {@code true}, as neither - * block may dominate the other. + * If this block and {@code dom} have different parent bodies, this method first + * repeatedly replaces this block with its {@link #ancestorBlock() nearest ancestor} block until: + *
    + *
  • {@code null} is reached, in which case this method returns {@code false}; or
    • + *
  • both blocks are in the same parent body, in which case + * dominance is tested within that body.
    • + *
+ * + * @apiNote + * The method {@link Body#immediateDominators()} can be used to test for dominance, by repeatedly querying a block's + * immediately dominating block until {@code null} or {@code dom} is reached. @@ -381,0 +380,2 @@ + * @see Body#immediateDominators() + * @see Value#isDominatedBy @@ -432,3 +433,5 @@ - * Returns the immediate post dominator of this block, otherwise {@link Body#IPDOM_EXIT} if this block is the - * only block with no successors or if this block is one of many blocks that has no successors. - * Both this block and the immediate post dominator (if defined) have the same parent body. + * Returns the immediate post dominator of this block. + *

+ * If this block has no successors then this method returns the synthetic block + * {@link Body#IPDOM_EXIT} representing the synthetic exit used to compute + * the immediate post dominators. @@ -436,2 +439,5 @@ - * The post immediate dominator is the unique block that strictly post dominates this block, but does not strictly - * post dominate any other block that strictly post dominates this block. + * Both this block and the immediate post dominator (if defined) have the same parent body, + * except for the synthetic block {@link Body#IPDOM_EXIT}. + *

+ * The immediate post dominator is the unique block that strictly post dominates this block, + * but does not strictly post dominate any other block that strictly post dominates this block. @@ -439,1 +445,1 @@ - * @return the immediate dominator of this block, otherwise {@code null} if this block is the entry block. + * @return the immediate post dominator of this block, otherwise {@code Body#IPDOM_EXIT}. @@ -442,4 +448,0 @@ - if (this == ancestorBody().entryBlock()) { - return null; - } - @@ -553,1 +555,1 @@ - * Adds a new block to the parent body. + * Creates a new sibling block in the same parent body as this block. @@ -563,1 +565,1 @@ - * Adds a new block to the parent body. + * Creates a new sibling block in the same parent body as this block. @@ -600,2 +602,2 @@ - public Reference successor(Value... args) { - return successor(List.of(args)); + public Reference reference(Value... args) { + return reference(List.of(args)); @@ -612,1 +614,1 @@ - public Reference successor(List args) { + public Reference reference(List args) { @@ -614,1 +616,1 @@ - throw new IllegalStateException("Entry block cannot be referred to as a successor"); + throw new IllegalStateException("Entry block cannot be referenced and used as a successor");

When a body is {@link Body.Builder#build(Op) built} all blocks + * A body is built using a {@link Body.Builder body builder} that creates and {@link Builder#entryBlock() exposes} an + * entry {@link Block.Builder block builder} from which further non-entry sibling blocks in the body may be created and + * {@link Block.Builder#block(TypeElement...) built}. A block builder can also be used to + * {@link Block.Builder#reference(Value...) create} references to non-entry sibling blocks that can be used as + * successors of terminal operations. + * + * When a body is {@link Body.Builder#build(Op) built} all blocks @@ -117,3 +121,4 @@ - * Returns the body's function type. - *

The function type is composed of the body's entry block parameter types and - * the body's yield type. + * Returns the body's signature, represented as a function type. + *

+ * The signature's return type is the body's yield type and its parameter types are the + * body's entry block parameter types, in order. @@ -121,1 +126,1 @@ - * @return the body type. + * @return the body's signature. @@ -123,1 +128,1 @@ - public FunctionType bodyType() { + public FunctionType bodySignature() { @@ -249,2 +254,3 @@ - * A synthetic block representing the post dominator of all blocks - * when two or more blocks in the body have no successors. + * A synthetic exit block used when computing immediate post dominators. + * It represents the post dominator of all blocks when two or more blocks + * in the body have no successors. @@ -253,3 +259,2 @@ - * one block that has no successors. When there are two or more blocks - * with no successors then this block represents the immediate post - * dominator of those blocks + * one block with no successors. When a body has two or more blocks + * with no successors then this block acts as the single exit point. @@ -380,28 +385,0 @@ - /** - * Returns {@code true} if this body is dominated by the given body {@code dom}. - *

- * A body, {@code b} say, is dominated by {@code dom} if {@code b} is the same as {@code dom} or a descendant of - * {@code dom}. Specifically, if {@code b} and {@code dom} are not equal then {@code b} becomes the nearest ancestor - * body, the result of {@code b.parentOp().parentBlock().parentBody()}, and so on until either: - * {@code b == dom}, therefore {@code b} is dominated by {@code dom} and this method returns {@code true}; - * or {@code b.parentOp().parentBlock() == null}, therefore {@code b} is not dominated - * by {@code dom} and this method returns {@code false}. - * - * @param dom the dominating body - * @return {@code true} if this body is dominated by the given body {@code dom}. - */ - public boolean isDominatedBy(Body dom) { - return isDominatedBy(this, dom); - } - - static boolean isDominatedBy(Body r, Body dom) { - while (r != dom) { - r = r.ancestorBody(); - if (r == null) { - return false; - } - } - - return true; - } - @@ -462,2 +439,2 @@ - * @param ancestorBody the nearest ancestor body builder, may be null if isolated - * @param bodyType the body's function type + * @param ancestorBody the nearest ancestor body builder, may be null if isolated + * @param bodySignature the body's signature @@ -468,1 +445,1 @@ - public static Builder of(Builder ancestorBody, FunctionType bodyType) { + public static Builder of(Builder ancestorBody, FunctionType bodySignature) { @@ -470,1 +447,1 @@ - return of(ancestorBody, bodyType, CodeContext.create(), CodeTransformer.COPYING_TRANSFORMER); + * Operations contain child bodies, bodies contain child blocks, and blocks contain child operations, to form + * a tree of arbitrary depth. @@ -156,0 +159,1 @@ + * @see #isDescendantOf @@ -167,0 +171,13 @@ + /** + * Returns true if this element is a descendant of the ancestor element. + * + * @param ancestor the ancestor element. + * @return true if this element is a descendant of the ancestor element. + * @throws IllegalStateException if an unbuilt block is encountered. + * @see #isAncestorOf + */ + default boolean isDescendantOf(CodeElement ancestor) { + Objects.requireNonNull(ancestor); + return ancestor.isAncestorOf(this); + } +

@@ -29,2 +29,9 @@ - * A code item, one of {@link CodeElement}, {@link Value}, or {@link TypeElement}, is - * an item in a code model. + * A code item, one of {@link CodeElement}, {@link Value value}, + * {@link Block.Reference block reference}, or {@link TypeElement type element}, + * is an item in a code model. + *

+ * Code elements form a tree. + * Values and block references form graph-like relationships + * (dependency/use graphs and control flow graphs, respectively). + * Type elements classify values. + * @@ -34,1 +41,1 @@ - permits CodeElement, Value, TypeElement, Block.Reference { + permits CodeElement, Value, Block.Reference, TypeElement {

Such run time values are commonly referred to as captured values. @@ -597,2 +607,2 @@ - * @return the quoted code model, or an empty optional if the functional interface instance is not the result of a - * reflectable lambda expression or method reference. + * @return the quoted instance containing the code model, or an empty optional if the functional interface instance + * is not the result of a reflectable lambda expression or method reference.

@@ -6,1 +6,1 @@
- * A type, that defines a set of values.
+ * A code item that classifies values.
@@ -8,2 +8,4 @@
- * A type can be assigned to a {@link Value value} in a code model,
- * and implies the value is a member of the type's set.
+ * A {@link Value value}, one of {@link Block.Parameter} or {@link Op.Result}, has a
+ * {@code TypeElement} classifying that value.
+ * A {@link Body} has a {@code TypeElement}, the {@link Body#yieldType yield type},
+ * classifying values yielded from the body.
@@ -11,2 +13,2 @@
- * The {@code equals} method should be used to check if two type elements
- * are equal to each other.
+ * The {@code equals} method should be used to compare type elements.
+ *
@@ -14,2 +16,7 @@
- * Code model types enable reasoning statically about a code model,
- * approximating run time behaviour.
+ * Type elements enable reasoning statically about a code model, approximating
+ * run time behaviour.
+ *
+ * @see Value
+ * @see Block.Parameter
+ * @see Op.Result
+ * @see Body#yieldType()

@@ -140,1 +140,1 @@ - throw new IllegalStateException("Users are are unbuilt"); + throw new IllegalStateException("Users are unbuilt"); @@ -149,2 +149,3 @@ - * If {@code v} and {@code dom} are in not declared in the same block then, domination is the result of - * if the declaring block of {@code v} is dominated by the declaring block of {@code dom}. + * If this value and {@code dom} are declared in different blocks, then this method + * returns the result of testing whether this value's declaring block is + * {@link Block#isDominatedBy dominated by} {@code dom}'s declaring block. @@ -152,1 +153,1 @@ - * Otherwise, if {@code v} and {@code dom} are declared in the same block then (in order): + * Otherwise, if both values are declared in the same block then: @@ -154,4 +155,5 @@ - *
  • if {@code dom} is a block parameter, then {@code v} is dominated by {@code dom}. - *
  • if {@code v} is a block parameter, then {@code v} is not dominated by {@code dom}. - *
  • otherwise, both {@code v} and {@code dom} are operation results, then {@code v} is dominated by {@code dom} - * if {@code v} is the same as {@code dom} or {@code v} occurs after {@code dom} in the declaring block. + *
  • if {@code dom} is a block parameter, then this value is dominated by {@code dom}. + *
  • if this value is a block parameter, then this value is not dominated by {@code dom}. + *
  • otherwise, both values are operation results, then this value is dominated by + * {@code dom} if this value is the same as {@code dom} or this value's operation occurs + * after {@code dom}'s operation in the declaring block. @@ -163,0 +165,1 @@ + * @see Block#isDominatedBy

-/// use. A value has a [_type element_][jdk.incubator.code.TypeElement], another code item, modeling the set of all
-/// possible values. In our example many of the type elements model Java types, and some model the type of variable
-/// values (the type element of the operation result of a var operation). In summary a code model contains five kinds of
-/// code item, operation, body, block, value, and type element.
